new_classes.label_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol < 80% vol; spirits, liqueurs and other spirituous beverages (CN 2208)
new_classes.label_de: Ethylalkohol < 80 % vol, unvergällt; Branntwein, Likör und andere alkoholhaltige Getränke (KN 2208)
new_classes.definition_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol of an alcoholic strength by volume of less than 80% vol; spirits, liqueurs and other spirituous beverages. Spirits, liqueurs and other spirituous beverages of heading 2208 are alcoholic liquids generally intended for human consumption and are prepared either directly by distilling (with or without added flavouring) fermented products or by mixing a distillate with other beverages, plants, or aromatic substances, or by fermentation.

new_classes.label_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol with alcoholic strength < 80% vol quality
new_classes.label_de: Ethylalkohol mit einem Alkoholgehalt von weniger als 80 % vol, unvergällt – Eigenschaft
new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by an ethyl alcohol product that is undenatured (not rendered unfit for human consumption) and has an alcoholic strength by volume of less than 80% vol.
A process in which fermented materials are distilled (with or without added flavouring) to produce spirits, liqueurs, or other spirituous beverages as covered by CN heading 2208.

coverage_explanation:
The core distinguishing criteria of CN heading 2208 are captured:
1. The product is an undenatured ethyl alcohol or spirituous beverage with alcoholic strength < 80% vol — modelled via the new quality class and a decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent (maxExclusive 80.0).
2. The product is intended for human consumption and produced by distillation — modelled via the new SpiritousDistillationProcess class linked through producedBy.
3. Disjointness from the two most closely related sibling wine headings (2204, 2205) is indicated.

Coverage gaps (reducing score from 1.0):
- The legal text also mentions preparation by mixing distillate with other beverages/plants/aromatic substances, or by fermentation without distillation (e.g. liqueurs produced by mixing). This alternative production pathway cannot be fully captured without additional process classes and further legal-text detail.
- The 'complement' restrictions were used to indicate disjointness with sibling wine classes, but the restriction structure here uses isDenatured as a placeholder property which is semantically incorrect for the complement pattern — proper owl:disjointWith axioms require a different modelling approach not directly supported by the restriction schema provided. This is a modelling limitation.
- The TBox does not include named individuals for measure types or footnotes relevant to 2208, so no regulatory measure axioms can be attached.
